description: Multi-model AI review workflow. AI models propose issues, cross-review each other's findings, and track confidence levels. Designed for code audits, framework reviews, and spec validation.

fields.name: Status
fields.description: Standard workflow status. All AI-generated issues start at Proposed.

options.name: Proposed
options.description: Suggested by a model but not yet approved by a human for work.

options.name: Todo
options.description: Approved by a human and queued for implementation.

options.name: In Progress
options.description: Actively being worked on.

options.name: Done
options.description: Fix has been implemented and verified.

fields.name: Model
fields.description: Which AI model originally found/proposed the issue. Set once at creation, never changed.

options.name: Claude
options.description: Anthropic Claude

options.name: Gemini
options.description: Google Gemini

options.name: Codex
options.description: OpenAI Codex

fields.name: Issue Type
fields.description: Classifies the nature of the work item.

options.name: Requirement
options.description: Feature or capability defined during PRD. Represents a user-facing deliverable.

options.name: Enhancement
options.description: Improvement to existing functionality. Not a new feature, not a bug fix.

options.name: Bug
options.description: Runtime defect — code crashes, produces wrong results, or behaves incorrectly.

options.name: Task
options.description: Implementation work that isn't a feature or fix (setup, refactoring, CI, docs).

options.name: Code Bug
options.description: Code error that would crash or produce clearly wrong behavior at runtime.

options.name: Math Bug
options.description: Formula or mathematical error that silently produces wrong numerical results without crashing.

options.name: Spec Deviation
options.description: Code differs from spec but neither is necessarily wrong — needs human review.

options.name: Doc Fix
options.description: Wording, naming, or clarity issue in the document. Codebase is fine.

fields.name: Confidence Level
fields.description: Proposing model's self-assessed confidence (1-10). 9-10: objectively verifiable. 7-8: strong evidence. 5-6: debatable. 3-4: mild concern. 1-2: cosmetic.

fields.name: Claude Reviewer
fields.description: Claude's assessment of another model's issue. Left blank for Claude-authored issues.

options.name: Agreed
options.description: Fully agrees the issue is valid and proposed fix is correct.

options.name: Mostly Agreed
options.description: Agrees issue exists but has reservations. Should leave a comment.

options.name: Disagree
options.description: Does not believe issue is valid. Should leave a comment with reasoning.

fields.name: Gemini Reviewer
fields.description: Gemini's assessment of another model's issue. Left blank for Gemini-authored issues.

options.name: Agreed
options.description: Fully agrees.

options.name: Mostly Agreed
options.description: Agrees with reservations.

options.name: Disagree
options.description: Does not agree.

fields.name: Codex Reviewer
fields.description: Codex's assessment of another model's issue. Left blank for Codex-authored issues.

options.name: Agreed
options.description: Fully agrees.

options.name: Mostly Agreed
options.description: Agrees with reservations.

options.name: Disagree
options.description: Does not agree.

fields.name: Consensus
fields.description: Auto-computed. Yes if all 3 fully Agree. Partial if all reviewed but some Mostly Agreed. No if any Disagree. Blank until all reviewed.

options.name: Yes
options.description: All 3 reviewers fully Agreed.

options.name: Partial
options.description: All reviewed, no Disagree, but not all fully Agreed (some Mostly Agreed).

options.name: No
options.description: At least one reviewer disagrees.

views_manual._note: GitHub API does not support view configuration. These define the intended view setup — must be applied manually in the UI after project creation.

rules.issue_body_requirements: Include full code snippets, line numbers, mathematical explanations, and impact analysis — enough for another AI model to independently evaluate the claim.
